note_data.title: Sets and Functions
keyPoints: Set is a well-defined collection of distinct objects called elements or members
Function is a special type of relation where each element in domain has exactly one image in codomain
Domain is the set of all first elements (x-values) of ordered pairs in a function
Range is the set of all second elements (y-values) that are actually used in a function
Codomain is the set from which the second elements of ordered pairs are taken
One-to-one function means no two different elements of domain have same image
Onto function means range equals codomain - every element of codomain is used
Bijective function is both one-to-one and onto function
Composite function (f∘g)(x) = f(g(x)) - apply g first, then f to the result

definitions.definition: A function is a relation in which each element of the domain is paired with exactly one element of the codomain
The domain of a function is the set of all first components (x-values) of the ordered pairs
The range of a function is the set of all second components (y-values) that are actually used
A function is called one-to-one if different elements of the domain have different images in the codomain
A function is called onto if every element of the codomain is the image of at least one element of the domain

importantQuestions.question: Define function and give an example.
importantQuestions.answer: A function is a relation in which each element of the domain is paired with exactly one element of the codomain. Example: f(x) = 2x + 3

importantQuestions.question: Let f(x) = 3x - 2 and g(x) = x² + 1. Find (f∘g)(x) and (g∘f)(x). Also find their domains.
importantQuestions.answer: (f∘g)(x) = f(g(x)) = f(x² + 1) = 3(x² + 1) - 2 = 3x² + 3 - 2 = 3x² + 1. (g∘f)(x) = g(f(x)) = g(3x - 2) = (3x - 2)² + 1 = 9x² - 12x + 4 + 1 = 9x² - 12x + 5. Domain of both composite functions is R (all real numbers).

mcqs.question: Which of the following is NOT a function?
If f(x) = x - 1 and g(x) = x + 2, then (f∘g)(3) = ?

commonMistakes: Students confuse domain and range - remember domain is input (x-values), range is output (y-values)
In composite functions, students apply functions in wrong order - (f∘g)(x) means f(g(x)), apply g first
Students think all relations are functions - a function must pass vertical line test
Confusing range and codomain - range is subset of codomain
Not writing function notation properly - always write f(x) = instead of just y =
examTips: This chapter usually has 2-3 MCQs and 1 long question in every BSEK paper
Always draw arrow diagrams for function types - examiners expect proper diagrams
Memorize exact definitions word-by-word as they appear in textbook
Practice composite function calculations - very common in numericals
Learn to identify function types (one-to-one, onto, bijective) from given sets
formulas: If f(x) = ax + b, then f⁻¹(x) = (x - b)/a
note_data.keyDiagram: Arrow diagram showing mapping between Domain and Codomain sets, with arrows pointing from each domain element to exactly one codomain element. Label Domain, Codomain, Range, and show examples of one-to-one, onto, and many-to-one mappings.
